signals.headline: **Key Developments**: President Trump stated the Iran war is "very close to over," with a second round of ceasefire talk
signals.raw: **Key Developments**: President Trump stated the Iran war is "very close to over," with a second round of ceasefire talks possibly in Islamabad within two days, amid an "in principle agreement" by Washington and Tehran to extend the current two-week ceasefire past April 22 for more negotiations.[1][2][3]

signals.headline: **Blockade Context**: US naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz (launched April 13) was breached by Iranian supertankers
signals.raw: **Blockade Context**: US naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz (launched April 13) was breached by Iranian supertankers and cargo ships on April 14, signaling fragile enforcement during the ceasefire.[1][2][5]

signals.raw: **Conflicting Notes**: First talks in Islamabad ended inconclusively; some reports call them "collapsed," while Trump contrasts prior "slow" assessments with new optimism—prior skepticism persists on reconciling red lines.[3][4][5]
